queries.name: Intel vs AMD Performance Baseline Comparison with Cost Analysis
queries.pain_point: Lack of visibility into workload characteristics by hardware platform - cannot identify migration candidates or quantify performance differences
queries.description: Establish performance baselines across identical workloads on Intel Xeon vs AMD EPYC architectures. Calculate performance-per-dollar and cost-per-transaction metrics to quantify TCO differences. Filter to last 30 days of standardized benchmark runs.
queries.query:
FROM infrastructure_metrics
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30 days AND test_phase == "baseline"
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| LOOKUP JOIN applications ON application_id
| LOOKUP JOIN benchmark_definitions ON benchmark_id
| STATS 
    avg_transaction_duration_ms = AVG(transaction.duration.us) / 1000,
    p95_transaction_duration_ms = PERCENTILE(transaction.duration.us, 95) / 1000,
    avg_tps = AVG(transactions_per_second),
    avg_cpu_pct = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct),
    avg_cost_per_hour = AVG(compute_hosts.hourly_compute_cost_usd),
    avg_power_watts = AVG(compute_hosts.power_consumption_tdp_watts),
    test_count = COUNT(*)
  BY compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, compute_hosts.host.cpu.model.name, applications.workload_category, benchmark_definitions.benchmark_type
| EVAL 
    performance_per_dollar = avg_tps / avg_cost_per_hour,
    cost_per_million_transactions = (avg_cost_per_hour / avg_tps) * 1000000,
    power_efficiency = avg_tps / avg_power_watts
| SORT compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, applications.workload_category, performance_per_dollar DESC

queries.name: Application-Specific Migration Prioritization with ROI Calculation
queries.pain_point: Application-Specific Migration Prioritization - identify which applications benefit most from AMD migration
queries.description: Compare each application's performance on Intel vs AMD to identify best migration candidates. Use INLINESTATS to calculate architecture-specific averages, then rank applications by potential performance gain and cost savings. Focus on applications not yet migrated.
queries.query:
FROM infrastructure_metrics
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30 days AND test_phase == "baseline"
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| LOOKUP JOIN applications ON application_id
| WHERE applications.migration_status != "completed"
| INLINESTATS 
    avg_tps_by_arch = AVG(transactions_per_second),
    avg_cost_by_arch = AVG(cost_per_hour_usd)
  BY applications.application_id, compute_hosts.cpu_architecture
| STATS 
    intel_avg_tps = AVG(CASE(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == "Intel", transactions_per_second, null)),
    amd_avg_tps = AVG(CASE(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == "AMD", transactions_per_second, null)),
    intel_avg_cost = AVG(CASE(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == "Intel", cost_per_hour_usd, null)),
    amd_avg_cost = AVG(CASE(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == "AMD", cost_per_hour_usd, null)),
    current_architecture = VALUES(CASE(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == "Intel", "Intel", null))
  BY applications.application_id, applications.application_name, applications.business_unit, applications.criticality_tier
| WHERE intel_avg_tps IS NOT NULL AND amd_avg_tps IS NOT NULL
| EVAL 
    performance_gain_pct = ((amd_avg_tps - intel_avg_tps) / intel_avg_tps) * 100,
    cost_reduction_pct = ((intel_avg_cost - amd_avg_cost) / intel_avg_cost) * 100,
    annual_savings_usd = (intel_avg_cost - amd_avg_cost) * 24 * 365,
    migration_score = (performance_gain_pct * 0.6) + (cost_reduction_pct * 0.4)
| WHERE performance_gain_pct > 0 AND cost_reduction_pct > 0
| SORT migration_score DESC
| LIMIT 50

queries.name: Real-Time Performance Regression Detection with Change Point Analysis
queries.pain_point: Continuous Performance Regression Detection - automatically detect performance regressions after infrastructure changes
queries.description: Detect statistically significant performance regressions in real-time using CHANGE_POINT analysis. Aggregate transaction duration by hour, identify change points, and flag SLA violations. Essential for catching issues during migration windows.
queries.query:
FROM infrastructure_metrics
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 7 days
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| LOOKUP JOIN applications ON application_id
| EVAL hour_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 hour, @timestamp)
| STATS 
    avg_duration_ms = AVG(transaction.duration.us) / 1000,
    p95_duration_ms = PERCENTILE(transaction.duration.us, 95) / 1000,
    p99_duration_ms = PERCENTILE(transaction.duration.us, 99) / 1000,
    error_rate_pct = (COUNT(CASE(event.outcome == "failure", 1, null)) / COUNT(*)) * 100,
    avg_cpu_pct = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct)
  BY hour_bucket, applications.application_id, applications.application_name, applications.sla_target_ms, compute_hosts.hostname, compute_hosts.cpu_architecture
| SORT hour_bucket ASC
| CHANGE_POINT avg_duration_ms BY hour_bucket
| WHERE change_point_detected == true OR p95_duration_ms > applications.sla_target_ms
| EVAL 
    sla_violation = CASE(p95_duration_ms > applications.sla_target_ms, "YES", "NO"),
    severity = CASE(
      p99_duration_ms > applications.sla_target_ms * 2, "CRITICAL",
      p95_duration_ms > applications.sla_target_ms * 1.5, "HIGH",
      "MEDIUM"
    )
| SORT hour_bucket DESC
| LIMIT 100

queries.name: Unified Kafka and Infrastructure Performance Correlation
queries.pain_point: Consolidate Kafka & Hive Metrics into Unified Observability - bring fragmented data into Elasticsearch for real-time correlation
queries.description: Correlate Kafka consumer lag and throughput with underlying infrastructure metrics to identify bottlenecks. Use FUSE to combine Kafka-specific metrics with system-level performance data for holistic view.
queries.query:
FUSE
  (
    FROM kafka_metrics
    | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 24 hours
    | LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
    | STATS 
        avg_consumer_lag = AVG(consumer_lag),
        max_consumer_lag = MAX(consumer_lag),
        avg_throughput = AVG(message_throughput_per_sec),
        avg_broker_cpu = AVG(broker_cpu_pct)
      BY compute_hosts.hostname, compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, topic_name
    | EVAL metric_source = "kafka"
  ),
  (
    FROM infrastructure_metrics
    | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 24 hours
    | LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
    | STATS 
        avg_system_cpu = AVG(system.cpu.total.pct),
        avg_network_in_mb = AVG(system.network.in.bytes) / 1024 / 1024,
        avg_network_out_mb = AVG(system.network.out.bytes) / 1024 / 1024
      BY compute_hosts.hostname, compute_hosts.cpu_architecture
    | EVAL metric_source = "infrastructure"
  )

queries.name: Semantic Search for High-Risk Migration Candidates
queries.pain_point: No standardized performance benchmarking framework - ad-hoc testing with inconsistent tools and metrics across teams
queries.description: Use semantic search to identify applications with characteristics that make them high-risk for migration (e.g., 'latency-sensitive real-time trading applications with strict SLA requirements'). Enrich with current performance metrics to prioritize testing.
queries.query:
FROM applications
| WHERE SEMANTIC(application_characteristics, "compute-intensive batch processing applications with high CPU utilization that could benefit from AMD EPYC architecture")
| WHERE migration_status == "not_started" OR migration_status == "planning"
| LOOKUP JOIN infrastructure_metrics ON application_id
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| WHERE infrastructure_metrics.@timestamp > NOW() - 7 days
| STATS 
    avg_tps = AVG(infrastructure_metrics.transactions_per_second),
    avg_cpu = AVG(infrastructure_metrics.system.cpu.total.pct),
    current_arch = VALUES(compute_hosts.cpu_architecture)
  BY applications.application_id, applications.application_name, applications.criticality_tier
| SORT avg_cpu DESC
| LIMIT 25

queries.name: Cost-Performance TCO Analysis with Parameterized Architecture Comparison
queries.pain_point: Cost-Performance Analysis & TCO Modeling - quantify cost savings and calculate ROI for Intel-to-AMD migration
queries.description: Calculate comprehensive TCO including compute costs, licensing, and power consumption. Compare Intel vs AMD across business units. Parameterized query allows filtering by specific architecture for what-if analysis.
queries.query:
FROM infrastructure_metrics
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30 days AND compute_hosts.cpu_architecture == ?target_architecture
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| LOOKUP JOIN applications ON application_id
| LOOKUP JOIN cost_allocation ON applications.cost_center
| STATS 
    total_compute_hours = COUNT(*),
    avg_tps = AVG(transactions_per_second),
    total_compute_cost = SUM(cost_per_hour_usd),
    avg_power_consumption = AVG(power_consumption_watts)
  BY applications.business_unit, applications.cost_center, compute_hosts.cpu_architecture
| EVAL 
    annual_compute_cost = total_compute_cost * 12,
    annual_power_cost = (avg_power_consumption * 24 * 365 * 0.12) / 1000,
    licensing_cost = 50000,
    total_tco_annual = annual_compute_cost + annual_power_cost + licensing_cost,
    cost_per_million_transactions = (total_tco_annual / (avg_tps * 86400 * 365)) * 1000000
| SORT total_tco_annual DESC

queries.name: Multi-Dimensional Stress Test Analysis with FORK
queries.pain_point: Stress Test & Capacity Planning - determine maximum sustainable load and breaking points under stress conditions
queries.description: Use FORK to simultaneously analyze multiple dimensions of stress test results: peak performance, error rates under load, and resource saturation points. Identifies maximum sustainable capacity before SLA degradation.
queries.query:
FROM infrastructure_metrics
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 7 days AND test_phase == "stress_test"
| LOOKUP JOIN compute_hosts ON host_id
| LOOKUP JOIN applications ON application_id
| LOOKUP JOIN benchmark_definitions ON benchmark_id
| FORK
  (
    STATS 
      peak_tps = MAX(transactions_per_second),
      avg_tps = AVG(transactions_per_second),
      test_duration_hours = (MAX(@timestamp) - MIN(@timestamp)) / 3600000
    BY compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, applications.application_name, benchmark_definitions.target_load_level
    | EVAL analysis_type = "peak_performance"
  ),
  (
    STATS 
      error_rate_pct = (COUNT(CASE(event.outcome == "failure", 1, null)) / COUNT(*)) * 100,
      total_errors = COUNT(CASE(event.outcome == "failure", 1, null))
    BY compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, applications.application_name, benchmark_definitions.target_load_level
    | WHERE error_rate_pct > 0.1
    | EVAL analysis_type = "error_analysis"
  ),
  (
    STATS 
      max_cpu_pct = MAX(system.cpu.total.pct),
      max_memory_pct = MAX(system.memory.used.pct),
      saturation_point = AVG(CASE(system.cpu.total.pct > 80, transactions_per_second, null))
    BY compute_hosts.cpu_architecture, applications.application_name
    | WHERE max_cpu_pct > 80 OR max_memory_pct > 85
    | EVAL analysis_type = "resource_saturation"
  )
